body.title: Fill missing palace subsystem entries: pursuits, voice_ab, graph_calving
body.result: Added 3 palace nodes (pursuits, voice_ab, graph_calving) under theigors/subsystem_index/. Verified via SELECT updated_by='cap-map-followups'.
body.description:
Three subsystems are LIVE on this host (gates: IGOR_PURSUITS_ENABLED=true, IGOR_CALVING_ENABLED=true; voice_ab is unconditionally part of output pipeline) but had no palace subsystem_index node. A fresh session asking 'is X built?' had to grep code instead of reading the palace.

DONE 2026-04-27: Added palace nodes:
- theigors/subsystem_index/pursuits — Pursuit layer, primary cognition/pursuits.py
- theigors/subsystem_index/voice_ab — A/B voice production framework, primary cognition/voice_ab.py
- theigors/subsystem_index/graph_calving — memory-tree splitting, primary memory/cortex.py + tools/graph_ops.py

Affected files:
- clan.memory_palace (3 INSERT rows, updated_by='cap-map-followups')

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: each node points at primary file's top-of-file docstring as canonical.
- postgres-only: palace lives in Postgres ✓.
- no-new-memory-schemas: uses existing memory_palace table ✓.
- test-plan-or-why-not: verification IS the SQL SELECT showing new rows.

Scope boundary:
IN: 3 subsystem_index nodes + their content.
OUT: refactoring the subsystems themselves, moving code, updating top-of-file docstrings (separate ticket if needed).

Test plan:
- SELECT path FROM clan.memory_palace WHERE updated_by='cap-map-followups' returns the 3 expected paths plus narrative_engine + reading_worker_pool + turn_pipeline (covered by sibling tickets in this batch).
- Verified 2026-04-27: 6 rows returned, all 3 of this ticket's targets present.
